violations.text: Establishment's Rhode Island Manager Certified In Food Safety license is expired. PIC to reinstate. Application provided during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2026

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. All dishes to be sanitized in 3 bay sink until unit is properly adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

Parfaits at open air case were observed held at 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Time temperature abused foods were voluntarily disposed of. Ambient of open air unit was 45 degrees Fahrenheit. No foods that require refrigeration for food safety to be placed in unit until properly adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

1 door reach-in was observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods in unit were evaluated at the time of inspection and moved to colder refrigeration. Establishment is to not store any TCS foods in unit until unit is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or lower. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024
